featureName: BUG-006-006: Learning Path Video Duration Never Parsed (ISO 8601 vs seconds)

executionHistory.summary: improve trigger (stochastic-quality-sweep round 38) probed spec 006 learning-path estimator. Root-caused: estimateReadingTime parses metadata.duration as bare seconds, but the YouTube connector stores ISO 8601 (PT45M); every video silently defaults to 15 min. Planned a dedicated ISO 8601 + seconds parser.

Added videoDurationISO8601RE + parseVideoDurationSeconds to internal/intelligence/learning.go and rewired the YouTube branch of estimateReadingTime; preserved the bare-seconds path and the 15-min fallback.

Added adversarial TestEstimateReadingTime_YouTubeISO8601 (captured RED before fix: all ISO durations returned 15), plus TestEstimateReadingTime_YouTubePlainSecondsStillWorks and TestEstimateReadingTime_YouTubeUnparseableDuration. All GREEN after fix.

Full internal/intelligence package PASS (ok 0.094s) and full repo ./smackerel.sh test unit --go finished OK — no neighboring test regressed. Spec-scoped artifact-lint and traceability-guard clean for the delta.
Change boundary respected: only internal/intelligence/learning.go, internal/intelligence/learning_test.go, and this bug packet touched. No connector/schema/config/CI surface changed.
Bug certified done. All 7 DoD items checked with file:line evidence and RED/GREEN regression captures in report.md.
